citations.narrative:
In an enclosure housing 2 raccoons, there are approximately 3 to 5 cobwebs, approximately 2 inches X 1 inch in size in
the windowsills and ceiling corners, a moderate amount of dirt and debris collecting around the floor edges of the
enclosure, and the blanket in the hammock is soiled with at least 2 holes noted. The presence of cobwebs may trap dirt
and debris, the accumulation of debris in corners and a soiled blanket can increase the risk of disease hazards to animal
enclosures. Animal enclosures, including its contents, should be cleaned and maintained as required to minimize
contamination and reduce disease hazards and odors in animal areas.
To be corrected by February 11, 2026

A wooden perch in the red-tailed hawk enclosure is covered with turf. An approximate 3-inch section of this covering has
become unraveled. The bird could chew and/or ingest the material which could pose a health hazard to the bird.
Furniture-type objects, such as perches and other objects that enrich a bird's environment, must be species-appropriate
and be designed, constructed, and maintained so as to prevent harm to the bird.
To be corrected by February 11, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.131(a) 
   
Sanitation. 
In an enclosure housing 2 raccoons, there are approximately 3 to 5 cobwebs, approximately 2 inches X 1 inch in size in 
the windowsills and ceiling corners, a moderate amount of dirt and debris collecting around the floor edges of the 
enclosure, and the blanket in the hammock is soiled with at least 2 holes noted. The presence of cobwebs may trap dirt 
and debris, the accumulation of debris in corners and a soiled blanket can increase the risk of disease hazards to animal 
enclosures. Animal enclosures, including its contents, should be cleaned and maintained as required to minimize 
contamination and reduce disease hazards and odors in animal areas. 
To be corrected by February 11, 2026 
3.153(a)(2) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
A wooden perch in the red-tailed hawk enclosure is covered with turf. An approximate 3-inch section of this covering has 
become unraveled. The bird could chew and/or ingest the material which could pose a health hazard to the bird. 
Furniture-type objects, such as perches and other objects that enrich a bird's environment, must be species-appropriate 
and be designed, constructed, and maintained so as to prevent harm to the bird. 
To be corrected by February 11, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
